title: Li Liu (Cheng-Han)
comment: /* Service under Li Xiáng and Li Te */
diff_added: In 300, Li Liu's other brother [[Li Xiang (Xuanxu)|Li Xiáng]] (courtesy name ''Xuanxu'') joined a rebellion led by [[Zhao Xin (Jin dynasty)|Zhao Xin]] against the [[Jin dynasty (266–420)]] in Yizhou. Li Liu followed suit and gathered his own men to join his brother. However, Zhao Xin would kill Li Xiáng out of jealousy the following year. Zhao Xin sent messengers to console Li Liu and Li Te about their loss, but they only grew enraged. The two brothers led troops to [[Chengdu]] to avenge Li Xiáng, forcing Zhao Xin to flee from the province.<ref>(廞恐朝廷討己，遣長史費遠、犍為太守李苾、督護常俊督萬餘人斷北道，次綿竹之石亭。特密收合得七千餘人，夜襲遠軍，遠大潰，因放火燒之，死者十八九。進攻成都。廞聞兵至，驚懼不知所為。李苾、張征等夜斬關走出，文武盡散。廞獨與妻子乘小船走至廣都，為下人硃竺所殺。特至成都，縱兵大掠，害西夷護軍薑發，殺廞長史袁治及廞所置守長，遣其牙門王角、李基詣洛陽陳廞之罪狀。) Book of Jin, Volume 120</ref>

title: Curtain
diff_added: Curtains are often hung on the inside of a building's [[window]]s to block the passage of light. For instance, at [[night]] to aid [[sleep]]ing, or to stop light from escaping outside the building (stopping people outside from being able to see inside, often for privacy reasons). In this application, they are also known as ''draperies''. Curtains hung over a [[door]]way are known as [[portière]]s.<ref name="britannica-curtain">{{cite web |url= http://www.britannica.com/EBchecked/topic/147125/curtain/|title=Curtain|publisher=Encyclopædia Britannica|access-date=5 May 2012}}</ref> Curtains come in a variety of shapes, materials, sizes, colors, and patterns. They often have their own sections within [[department store]]s, while some shops are completely dedicated to selling curtains.

title: Black rat
diff_added: The '''black rat''' ('''''Rattus rattus'''''), also known as the '''roof rat''' or '''ship rat''', is a common long-tailed [[rodent]] of the stereotypical rat genus ''[[Rattus]]'', in the subfamily [[Murinae]].<ref name="iucn status 19 November 2021" /> It is a generalist [[omnivore]] that can live on a wide variety of foods. title: Cabinet of Morocco
diff_added:
The '''Cabinet of Morocco''' is the chief executive body of the [[Morocco|Kingdom of Morocco]]. It is composed of ministers, including ministers delegate, and is headed by the [[Prime Minister of Morocco|Prime Minister]].
Since the adoption of the 2011 Constitution, the Prime Minister, officially titled Head of Government, is appointed by the [[King of Morocco]] from the political party that won the most seats in the [[Elections in Morocco|parliamentary elections]]. The members of the Cabinet are proposed by the Head of Government, in consultation with coalition parties, and are subsequently appointed by the King.
As of October 2021, the Cabinet is headed by [[Aziz Akhannouch]], appointed by [[Mohammed VI of Morocco|King Mohammed VI]] following the [[2021 Moroccan general election]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.moroccoworldnews.com/2021/09/344390/aziz-akhannouch-moroccos-new-billionaire-prime-minister|title=Aziz Akhannouch: Morocco's New Billionaire Prime Minister|first=Shaquile|last=Goff|publisher=Morocco World News|date=19 September 2021|accessdate=18 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|title=Le Roi Mohammed VI nomme Aziz Akhannouch chef du gouvernement|language=fr-FR|website=Medias24|url=https://medias24.com/2021/09/10/le-roi-mohammed-vi-nomme-aziz-akhannouch-chef-du-gouvernement/|date=10 September 2021|accessdate=18 September 2021}}</ref> The current government includes 24 ministers, among them several ministers delegate, and was sworn in on 7 October 2021.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.aa.com.tr/en/middle-east/moroccan-king-names-new-government-headed-by-aziz-akhannouch/2386059|title=Moroccan king names new government headed by Aziz Akhannouch|publisher=Anadolu Agency|date=7 October 2021|accessdate=14 October 2021}}</ref>

== Cabinet of Aziz Akhannouch (2021–present) ==
The '''Cabinet of Aziz Akhannouch''' is the eleventh government of the Kingdom of Morocco. It was formed on 7 October 2021, following the [[2021 Moroccan general election]], and is headed by [[Aziz Akhannouch]] under the reign of [[Mohammed VI of Morocco|King Mohammed VI]].

The government is composed of ministers from a coalition of three political parties: the [[National Rally of Independents]] (RNI), the [[Authenticity and Modernity Party]] (PAM), and the [[Istiqlal Party]] (PI). It succeeded the Cabinet of [[Saadeddine Othmani|Saad-Eddine El Othmani]].

The formation of the government was accompanied by a reorganization of several ministries, including the renaming, merging, and redistribution of certain portfolios.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Voici la liste officielle des ministres du gouvernement Akhannouch |url=https://telquel.ma/2021/10/07/voici-la-liste-officielle-des-ministres-du-gouvernement-akhannouch_1739427?fbrefresh=5 |access-date=2022-06-02 |website=Telquel.ma |language=fr}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last= |first= |title=King Mohammed VI Receives New Ministers as Part of Government Reshuffle |url=https://www.moroccoworldnews.com/2024/10/365910/king-mohammed-vi-
diff_removed:
The '''Cabinet of Morocco''' is the chief executive body of the [[Morocco|Kingdom of Morocco]]. The Cabinet is composed of some 24 ministers including 5 "Minister Delegates". It is headed by the [[Prime Minister of Morocco|Prime Minister]] since August 2011, who is appointed by the [[King of Morocco]] from the [[List of political parties in Morocco|party]] that achieved a [[Plurality (voting)|plurality]] in the [[Elections in Morocco|parliamentary elections]]. The Cabinet's ministers are chosen by the PM, after consultation with other parties forming the Government coalition, then validated and appointed by the King. title: Hiroshi Hamaya
comment: Add sourced detail on Hamaya's participation in the 1938 Seinen Hodo Shashin Kenkyukai
diff_added:
Hamaya was born in [[Shitaya, Tokyo]], Japan, on 28 March 1915.<ref name="mihashi"/>

In 1938, he helped found the Seinen Hodo Shashin Kenkyukai, a youth reportage photography group formed with the support of ''[[Photo Times]]''.<ref name="TobunkenHamaya">{{cite web |script-title=ja:濱谷浩 |url=https://www.tobunken.go.jp/materials/bukko/28145.html |access-date=2026-03-25 |website=Tokyo National Research Institute for Cultural Properties |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="TOPMuseumAGRPress">{{cite web |script-title=ja:アヴァンガルド勃興　近代日本の前衛写真 |trans-title=Avant-Garde Rising: The Photographic Vanguard in Modern Japan |url=https://topmuseum.jp/upload/2/4300/%E5%89%8D%E8%A1%9B%E5%86%99%E7%9C%9F%E3%83%97%E3%83%AC%E3%82%B9%E3%83%AA%E3%83%AA%E3%83%BC%E3%82%B9_20220519.pdf |access-date=2026-03-25 |website=Tokyo Photographic Art Museum |language=ja |format=PDF}}</ref>

Between 1942 and 1945 he contributed to ''[[Front (Japanese magazine)|Front]]'', the propaganda photo journal of the Tōhō-sha company.<ref>{{cite magazine|title=Make Magazines, Not War |url=https://www.printmag.com/daily-heller/make-magazines-not-war/|access-date=10 September 2022|magazine=[[Print (magazine)|Print]]|date=20 May 2012}}</ref>

title: List of Hazel episodes
diff_added: | ShortSummary=Gus Jenkins ([[Patrick McVey]]), an old flame of Hazel's, writes her. The letter had been forwarded several times, but says she should write him if she would like to see him again. Hazel tells Dorothy how the two of them met. Hazel's not sure she should write him, but Dorothy says she'll never know how Gus feels if she doesn't. George tells Dorothy he doesn't think Hazel should hook up with a drifter, but Hazel does write him. Hazel keeps asking Barney if a letter came for her. A week later, Hazel gets a call from Gus. He will stop by to see her that evening. An excited Hazel gets ready for her date. Harold is afraid Hazel will stop loving him, but Dorothy explains that could never happen. On their evening out, Gus and Hazel go to a few of their old hangouts, but the places have all changed. At an amusement park, Gus asks Hazel to marry him. Meanwhile, Harold asks George where Hazel and Gus would live if they got married. Hazel explains to Gus that he would never be happy settling down. Gus knows she's right. He kisses Hazel goodbye and goes to the bus station. When she arrives home, Hazel assures Harold she'll be around for him.

title: 2025–26 Mid-American Conference men's basketball season
diff_added: The '''2025–26 Mid-American Conference men's basketball season''' began with practices in October 2025, followed by the start of the [[2025–26 NCAA Division I men's basketball season]] in November. Conference play will begin in December 2025 and conclude in March 2026. The [[2026 MAC Men's Basketball Tournament|2026 MAC tournament]] was held at [[Rocket Arena]] in [[Cleveland|Cleveland, Ohio]] for the 27th consecutive season.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.cleveland19.com/2021/03/12/mac-tournament-staying-downtown-cleveland-through/|title=MAC Tournament staying in downtown Cleveland through 2030|website=Cleveland 19 News|first=Tony|last=Zarrella|language=en|access-date=2022-05-26|date=March 12, 2021}}</ref>  The conference saw its first change in membership since [[2005–06 Mid-American Conference men's basketball season|2005]] with the addition of [[UMass Minutemen basketball|UMass]]<ref>{{cite web |url=https://apnews.com/article/umass-mac-realignment-519f806947a7f597cc783290546b585e|title=UMass will join Mid-American Conference as a full sports member in 2025, MAC commissioner says|website=APNews|first=Tom|last=Withers|language=en|access-date=2024-04-20|date=February 27, 2024}}</ref> It will also be the last MAC season for [[Northern Illinois Huskies men's basketball|Northern Illinois]], which will rejoin the [[Horizon League]] in 2026 after a nearly 30-year absence.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://horizonleague.org/news/2025/2/27/general-horizon-league-welcomes-niu.aspx |title=Horizon League Welcomes NIU |publisher=Horizon League |date=February 27, 2025 |access-date=June 6, 2025}}</ref>

title: Gary Franks
comment: Add books written (per COI edit request); move papers and foundation material to "Personal Life" section
diff_added:
==Political views==
Franks ran as a candidate in favor of [[welfare reform]] and an opponent of [[affirmative action]].  He was a supporter of the nomination of [[Clarence Thomas]] to the Supreme Court. He opposed increased taxation and supported a reduced [[capital gains tax]] and an amendment to ban desecration of the American flag.

Franks opposed the Civil Rights Act of 1990. One of his claims was that it would enforce quotas that would encourage companies to move out of Connecticut to states with a greater proportion of whites. However, he did vote for the [[Civil Rights Act of 1991]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.govtrack.us/congress/votes/102-1991/h386|title=S. 1745 (102nd): Civil Rights Act of 1991 -- House Vote #386 -- Nov 7, 1991|website=GovTrack.us|accessdate=November 28, 2018}}</ref>

After becoming the first Republican voting member of the [[Congressional Black Caucus]], Franks was ejected from the strategy sessions of the caucus on the claim he was a Republican mole.

During his time in Congress, Franks supported [[abortion]] rights. Years later, he changed his mind. In a 2022 ''[[Boston Herald]]'' column, Franks expressed concern particularly for black women, whom he considered to have a disproportionate number of abortions: "I supported the so-called [[Abortion-rights movements|pro-choice]] position while in Congress. These are votes I regret today and pray for God’s forgiveness on so many levels.... I implore the U.S. Supreme Court to do everything in its power to stop the madness. Socioeconomic reasons should not be justification to abort a pregnancy in America. We, as a society, are better than that."<ref>{{Cite web|date=January 28, 2022|title=Franks: High abortion rate strikes blow at Black community|url=https://www.bostonherald.com/2022/01/28/franks-high-abortion-rate-strikes-blow-at-black-community/|access-date=February 9, 2022|website=Boston Herald|language=en-US}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Franks married Donna Williams in 1990; they have two daughters and a son. He is a [[Baptists in the United States|Baptist]].
== Books written==
Franks is the author of several books. In 1996, he published ''Searching for the Promised Land: An African American's Optimistic Odyssey'' with ReganBooks, an imprint of HarperCollins.<ref>{{cite book
|last=Franks
|first=Gary
|title=Searching for the Promised Land: An African American's Optimistic Odyssey
|publisher=ReganBooks
|location=New York
|year=1996
|isbn=9780060391560
}}</ref> In 2016, he self-published two books, ''With God, For God and For Country: Because We Can Do Better'' and ''The Political Aberrations''.<ref>{{cite web
|title=Gary Franks – Author Page
|url=https://www.amazon.com/stores/Gary-Franks/author/B001HOPYCC
|website=Amazon
|access-date=23 March 2026
}}</ref>

The '''Institute of Management Technology-Dubai''' (IMTD) is an international business school. It is certified by the Ministry of Higher Education, [[United Arab Emirates|UAE]]; the institute is licensed by [[Ministry of higher education|MOHESR]] and [[Knowledge and Human Development Authority|KHDA]], and all its programs are accredited by the CAA.
It offers accredited undergraduate and postgraduate programs to students from a multitude of nationalities and educational backgrounds. Established in 2006, the institute was among the first International Business Schools to have set up a full-fledged campus in Dubai.

title: Oregon, Pacific, & Eastern 19
comment: Please don’t change image, because the previous image has higher quality
diff_added:
| name = McCloud Railway 19
| image = Yw nov 1 2008 (8096137127).jpg
| caption = No. 19 hauling a flatcar train on the Yreka Western, November 1, 2008
| retiredate = June 8, 1953
| restoredate = 1955
'''McCloud Railway 19''' is a preserved {{whyte|2-8-2}} "Mikado" type [[steam locomotive]], built in April 1915 by the [[Baldwin Locomotive Works]] (BLW) for the [[Caddo and Choctaw Railroad]] (C&CRR) in Arkansas. The locomotive also operated for the United States Smelting, Refining and Mining Company (USSR&MC), [[McCloud River Railroad]] (MCR), [[Yreka Western Railroad]] (YW), and the [[Oregon, Pacific and Eastern Railway|Oregon, Pacific, and Eastern Railway]] (OPE). The engine operated in Mexico before being sold to the [[McCloud River Railroad]] and then to the [[Yreka Western Railroad]], before being moved up to [[Cottage Grove, Oregon]] to run on the OP&E in excursion service. In the late 1980s, No. 19 was sent back down to [[Yreka, California]] to run on the YW after the financial collapse of the OP&E. In April 1994, the engine returned to [[McCloud, California]] to run a series of excursions on the [[McCloud Railway]]. It has since been used in the films ''[[Emperor of the North Pole]]'' and ''[[Stand by Me (film)|Stand By Me]]''. As of 2026, No. 19 is operational at the [[Age of Steam Roundhouse]] in [[Sugarcreek, Ohio]].
In 2020, restoration officially began to return the engine to operating condition.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hayhurst |first=Leonard L.|title=All aboard for the Age of Steam Roundhouse |url=https://www.coshoctontribune.com/story/news/local/2020/08/23/all-aboard-age-steam-roundhouse/3401830001/|access-date=2022-11-09 |website=Coshocton Tribune |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Trains.com2"/> On March 16, 2026, No. 19's overhaul was complete and made its first test run.<ref>{{Cite web |title=McCloud River Railroads News Page |url=http://mccloudriverrailroad.com/News.htm |access-date=July 11, 2025 |website=www.mccloudriverrailroad.com}}</ref> On October 11, 2025, it was announced that No. 19 will haul its first excursion train on April 9, 2026 for a three day grand steam christening event.<ref name="Trains.com2">{{Cite web |author=Trains Staff |title=Age of Steam Roundhouse’s 2-8-2 No. 19 to receive christening in April |url=https://www.trains.com/trn/railroads/heritage-rail/preservation/age-of-steam-roundhouses-2-8-2-no-19-to-receive-christening-in-april/ |date=October 11, 2025 |access-date=October 13, 2025 |website=Trains.com |language=en-US}}</ref>
llm_reason: The edit significantly changes the subject of the article from the Oregon, Pacific & Eastern Railway to the McCloud Railway 19. This suggests a potential attempt to redirect or misrepresent information. The added text also includes new details about the locomotive's history and builder, which require verification.

diff_added: O'Connor was born in [[Bray]] and joined English club [[Stoke City F.C.|Stoke City]] in 1996 after being spotted by the club's Irish base scouts. He made his debut in the [[1998–99 Stoke City F.C. season|1998–99]] season before becoming a regular in the team in [[1999–2000 Stoke City F.C. season|1999–2000]]. He scored eight goals in 55 appearances as Stoke reached the play-offs where they lost to [[Gillingham F.C.|Gillingham]] although he did play in the [[2000 Football League Trophy Final]] where Stoke beat [[Bristol City F.C.|Bristol City]] 2–1 and his performances during the season earn him the [[Stoke City F.C. He played 54 times in [[2000–01 Stoke City F.C. season|2000–01]] for Stoke scoring ten goals as they again failed in the play-offs losing to [[Walsall F.C.|Walsall]]. He played in 50 matches in [[2001–02 Stoke City F.C. season|2001–02]] as Stoke finally won promotion via the play-offs, beating [[Brentford F.C.|Brentford]] 2–0 in the [[2002 Football League Second Division play-off final]]. title: The Wall (SoHo)
diff_added: In September 2004 the owners were sued by the city government to replace the wall. [[US District Court]] Judge Deborah Batts threw out the initial defense by the owners that the wall was an infringement of their [[First Amendment rights]]. In May 2005, however, Judge Batts ruled that the city was violating the owners' [[Fifth Amendment to the United States Constitution|Fifth Amendment rights]], and that the city could not force the owners to maintain the wall. However, if the city did decide to go through with an order to replace the wall, it would owe the owners fair compensation for the wall's construction.<ref>{{cite news|first=Ronda|last=Kaysen|title=Judge's roadblock to 'Wall' return|url=http://www.downtownexpress.com/de_106/judgesroadblockto.html|work=Downtown Express|publisher=Community Media, LLC.|date=2006-05-20|access-date=2007-04-23}}</ref>

title: Mugdho Mancha
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: In 25 January 2025 → On 25 January 2025
diff_added:
'''Mugdho Mancha''' ({{langx|bn|মুগ্ধ মঞ্চ|Mugdhô Môncô}}) is an outdoor cultural stage and event venue located in [[Uttara (town)|Uttara]], Dhaka, Bangladesh.
It was built at Botomool which is situated at the western end of Rabindra Sarani in Sector 7 and was named as '''Language Warrior Poet Mahbub Ul Alam Chowdhury Mancha''' in memory of [[Bengali language movement]] activist [[Mahbub Ul Alam Choudhury]] while organizing street drama in 2016.
The [[Dhaka North City Corporation]] later assured to construct a stage structure at the site following the demands of Gitanjali Lalitkala Academy founder director Mahbub Amin Mithu. In 2022, the structure was inaugurated as the '''Bangabandhu Mukto Mancha''' in memory of Bangabandhu [[Sheikh Mujibur Rahman]], the first [[president of Bangladesh]].
In 2016, Gitanjali Lalitkala Academy organized a street theatre festival at the Botomool in Rabindra Sarani, [[Uttara (town)|Uttara]], Dhaka, which was the first street theatre initiative organized in the area. At the time, the place where the street plays was staged was named in memory of [[Mahbub Ul Alam Choudhury]], an activist of the [[Bengali language movement]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.prothomalo.com/bangladesh/%E0%A6%89%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%B0%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%AF%E0%A6%BC-%E0%A6%AA%E0%A6%A5%E0%A6%A8%E0%A6%BE%E0%A6%9F%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%AF%E0%A7%8B%E0%A7%8E%E0%A6%B8%E0%A6%AC-%E0%A6%B6%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%B0%E0%A7%81|script-title=bn:উত্তরায় পথনাট্যোৎসব শুরু|trans-title=Street theatre festival begins in Uttara|language=bn|date=13 February 2016|access-date=15 October 2024|newspaper=[[Prothom Alo]]}}</ref> Due to the lack of infrastructure in Uttara, cultural organizations had been demanding the construction of an outdoor venue structure to host events. In 2019, a book fair, discussion meeting and cultural festival were held at the same venue from March 17 to March 26. During the program, Mahbub Amin Mithu, founder of Gitanjali Lalitkala Academy, proposed the construction of a stage infrastructure at the place, and he was assured of its implementation with support. Nazrul researcher [[Rafiqul Islam (educationist)|Rafiqul Islam]] advised to name the proposed stage structure after Bangabandhu [[Sheikh Mujibur Rahman]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.dailyjanakantha.com/entertainment/news/422207|script-title=bn:উত্তরায় প্রতিষ্ঠিত হচ্ছে বঙ্গবন্ধু মুক্তমঞ্চ!|trans-title=Bangabandhu Mukto Mancha is being established in Uttara!|language=bn|date=16 May 2019|access-date=15 October 2024|newspaper=[[Janakantha]]}}</ref> In 2020, Uttara's first cultural outdoor structure had started under the supervision of [[Dhaka North City Corporation]].<ref name="ত্রান" /> As of 2021, the construction was ongoing.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.banglatribune.com/others/capital-city/672514/%E0%A6%89%E0%A6%A4%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A4%E0%A6%B0%E0%A6%BE%E0%A7%9F-%E2%80%98%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%99%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%97%E0%A6%AC%E0%A6%A8%E0%A7%8D%E0%A6%A7%E0%A7%81-%E0%A6%AE%E0%A7%81%E0%A6%95%E0%

title: Amos N. Guiora
diff_added:
'''Amos Neuser Guiora''' is an [[Israeli-American]] professor of law at S. J. Quinney College of Law, [[University of Utah]], specializing in institutional complicity, enabling culture, and sexual assaults.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.upr.org/post/revisiting-crime-complicity-amos-guiora-tuesdays-access-utah |title=Revisiting the Crime of Complicity |publisher=[[NPR]] |date=January 15, 2019 |access-date=June 3, 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last1=Davis |first1=Jenny B. |url=https://www.abajournal.com/magazine/article/law-professor-Amos-Guiora-exposes-the-harms-of-enabler-complicity |title=Son of Holocaust Survivors Exposes the Harms of Bystander Complicity |work=ABA Journal |date=February 1, 2021 |access-date=May 3, 2021}}</ref> Guiora’s scholarship explores institutional complicity in relation to the victimization of young people by college sports coaches, trainers, doctors, and Catholic priests. As a result of this work, Guiora has become not just an academic but also an advocate for sexual assault victims.
After graduating from Case, Guiora returned to Israel and served in the [[Israeli Defense Forces]] [[Military Advocate General|Judge Advocate General]] Corps, attaining the rank of [[lieutenant colonel]].<ref name="Guiora"/>
Guiora is the Inaugural Chair of the University of Utah Independent Review Committee (Presidential Appointment).
Guiora's most recent book<ref>Armies of Enablers: Survivor Stories of Complicity and Betrayal in Sexual Assaults</ref> reflects his research on institutional complicity, enabling culture, and sexual assaults. He has spoken at (virtual) conferences in South Africa, the United Kingdom, the Netherlands, and the U.S. on sexual abuse of athletes (Safe Sports)  and sexual exploitation of children.
Guiora has published extensively both in the U.S. and Europe on issues related to national security, limits of interrogation, religion and terrorism, the limits of power, multiculturalism, and human rights. He is the author of several books, articles, and book chapters.

title: David Sklansky
diff_added: '''David Sklansky''' (December 22, 1947 – March 23, 2026) was an American professional [[poker]] player and author. An early writer on poker strategy, he was known for his mathematical approach to the game. His key work ''The Theory of Poker'' presents fundamental principles on which much later analysis is based. His new book, Autism and Poker, co-written with his fiance Todd Witteles, examines Todd and others' autism, and how the new breed of poker players are almost exclusively on the spectrum. It will be released during the first week of the World Series of Poker in June of 2026, when David Sklansky will posthumously be inducted into the Poker Hall of Fame.

diff_added: Wheeler was born in 1880, and attended [[Bedford School]] from 1893 to 1897.{{citation needed|date=September 2012}} He was commissioned as a [[second lieutenant]] on the unattached list on 20 January 1900 "with a view to [his] appointment on the [[Indian Staff Corps]]".<ref>{{London Gazette |issue=27155 |date=19 January 1900 |page=362 }}</ref> Posted to the Madras Command three months later,<ref>{{Cite newspaper The Times |title=Naval & Military intelligence|date=12 April 1900 | page=5 |issue=36114}}</ref> he was formally transferred to the [[Indian Staff Corps]] in april 1901,<ref>{{London Gazette |issue=27348 |date=1901-08-23 |page=5594 }}</ref> and served with the [[9th Gorkha Rifles|9th (Gurkha Rifle) Bengal Infantry]]. He was promoted to [[Lieutenant (British Army and Royal Marines)|lieutenant]] on 20 April 1902.<ref>{{London Gazette | issue=27469 | date=29 August 1902 |page=5608}}</ref>

title: Treasure Planet
diff_added:
''Treasure Planet'' premiered in [[Paris]] on November 6, 2002, and was released in the United States on November 27 by [[Walt Disney Pictures]]. It was the first film to be released simultaneously in regular and [[IMAX]] theaters.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Diorio |first=Carl |title=Big Bang for Disney's 'Planet' |newspaper=[[Variety (magazine)|Daily Variety]] |page=51 |date=January 25, 2002 |url=https://read-archive.variety.com/html5/reader/production/default.aspx?pubname=&edid=5efa0aa1-32ea-4e5a-9d8d-d2e2505f4a4d&pnum=53 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20260223014039/https://pages.pagesuite.com/7/e/7e8c2f76-a6bd-404e-8b28-ebe4e2cd95e1/page.pdf |url-status=live |archive-date=2026-02-23 }}</ref> The film was a [[box-office bomb|commercial failure]], earning $109 million worldwide against a budget of $140 million, though it received generally positive reviews from critics, was nominated for [[Academy Award for Best Animated Feature|Best Animated Feature]] at the [[75th Academy Awards]], and has since gained a [[cult following]].<ref name="Polygon">{{cite web |url=https://www.polygon.com/animation-cartoons/2020/7/21/21319228/treasure-planet-disney-cast-characters-making-of-box-office-bombs-animation |title=Treasure Planet was the rebellious, visionary Disney movie that didn't pay off |first=Petrana |last=Radulovic |date=July 21, 2020 |website=[[Polygon (website)|Polygon]] |access-date=July 21, 2020 |archive-date=July 23, 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200723014511/https://www.polygon.com/animation-cartoons/2020/7/21/21319228/treasure-planet-disney-cast-characters-making-of-box-office-bombs-animation |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://movieweb.com/best-cult-classic-animated-movies/|title=22 Animated Cult Classics Worth Checking Out|first=Nina|last=Phillips|date=January 11, 2023|website=MovieWeb|access-date=January 12, 2023|archive-date=January 12, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230112153254/https://movieweb.com/best-cult-classic-animated-movies/|url-status=live}}</ref>
Jim finds Silver below decks and allows him to escape; as a farewell gift, Silver gives Jim and Morph a handful of treasure he had salvaged from Flint's hoard, to have Jim help Sarah rebuild their inn. Back on Montressor, having helped Sarah rebuild the inn, and B.E.N. becoming a waiter. Doppler and Amelia habe married and started a family, and Jim, having matured under Silver's mentorship, has become a cadet at the Interstellar Academy.
* [[Joseph Gordon-Levitt]] as [[Jim Hawkins (character)|Jim Hawkins]], an adolescent, determined and courageous adventurer.
* [[Brian Murray (actor)|Brian Murray]] as [[Long John Silver|John Silver]], a cyborg bear-like alien who befriends Jim, who thrives under his guidance, even though he leads the impending mutiny on the ''RLS Legacy''.

title: Victor-Joseph François
diff_added: Naturalized Belgian under the Dutch government, he became secretary and later president of the medical commission of Hainaut. He supported the [[Belgian Revolution]] in 1830 and organized the [[Civil Guard]] in Mons. In 1832, he dealt with a severe [[cholera]] epidemic in the city of [[Mons, Belgium|Mons]]. He was one of the founders of one of the oldest Belgian scientific societies, the [[Society of Sciences, Arts and Letters of Hainaut]] established in 1833, and in 1841, he was among the founders of the [[Royal Academy of Medicine of Belgium]].<ref name=":0" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Historique de la Société |url=https://www.ssalh.eu/histoire.html |access-date=2024-12-27 |website=Société des Sciences, des Arts et des Lettres du Hainaut|language=fr}}</ref>

title: Dan Povenmire
comment: /* 1992–1998: The Simpsons, Rocko's Modern Life, and his animation career */
diff_added: Though Povenmire started on ''Rocko'' simply to occupy his downtime from ''The Simpsons'', he found the greater creative freedom he enjoyed on his temporary job compelling, and quit ''The Simpsons'' to work on ''Rocko'' full-time. There, he reunited with Jeff Marsh, this time as a writing partner;<ref name="AM1"/> Marsh claimed the crew hoped Povenmire's neatness would offset his own sloppy storyboarding.<ref name="411news"/> The pair developed a distinctive style characterized by characteristic musical numbers and chase scenes.<ref name="AM2">{{cite journal |author=Strike, Joe. |date=2008-02-01 |title=From Swampy & Dan Emerges ''Phineas and Ferb'' |url=http://mag.awn.com/index.php?ltype=pageone&category2=&article_no=3534&page=2 |url-status=dead |journal=Animation World Magazine |page=2 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090821165941/http://mag.awn.com/index.php?ltype=pageone&category2=&article_no=3534&page=2 |archive-date=2009-08-21 |access-date=2025-06-23 }}</ref> Povenmire and Marsh won an Environmental Achievement Award for a 1996 ''Rocko'' episode they had written.<ref name="media"/> In addition in their time on ''Rocko'', the duo would work for one episode of ''[[The Ren and Stimpy Show|Ren & Stimpy]]'' in the episode, "[[Big Flakes]]" as additional storyboard artist, working alongside future ''[[Madagascar (franchise)|Madagascar]]'' creator [[Tom McGrath (animator)|Tom McGrath]], who had done main storyboards for the episode.<ref name="BigFlakes">{{cite book |last1=Dobbs |first1=G. Michael |title=Escape – How Animation Broke into the Mainstream in the 1990s |date=2015 |publisher=BearManor Media |isbn=978-1593931100 |location=Orlando}}</ref> Once production for ''Rocko'' had ended, Povenmire would work as storyboard for ''[[Hey Arnold!]]'' and writer for ''[[The Angry Beavers]]'', as well as both storyboard and writer for ''[[CatDog]]'' for a brief while.

title: Drum memory
diff_removed:
Gustav Tauschek (born April 29, 1899 in Vienna, Austria — died February 14, 1945 in Zürich, Switzerland) was an Austrian information technology pioneer who spent the years 1922 to 1945 creating a wide range of advancements for calculating machines based on punched cards.

The IBM 650 Magnetic Drum Data-Processing Machine was an early digital computer manufactured by IBM during the mid-1950s. It holds the distinction of being the world's first computer to be mass-produced. Nearly 2,000 units were built before production ended in 1962, and it was the first computer to generate a significant profit.

title: Trionfi (cards)
comment: no substitute for actually reading the article

{{Short description|15th and 16th-century Italian playing card sets}}
'''Trionfi''' ({{IPA|it|triˈoɱfi|lang}}, '[[wikt:trionfi|triumph]]s') are 15th and 16th-century [[Italian playing card]] sets, all [[Trump (card games)|"trumps"]] rather than numbers, with [[allegorical]] content related to those used in [[Tarot card games|tarocchi games]]. The general English expression "[[Trump (card games)#Metaphorical uses|trump card]]" and the German "trumpfen" (in card games) have developed from the Italian "Trionfi".  Most cards feature the [[personification]] of a place or abstraction.

title: Isaac Alzamora
comment: clean up, [[WP:AWB/T|typo(s) fixed]]: However  → However, , Jurisprudence → jurisprudence
diff_added:
He was born on 3 June 1850 in [[Lima]], [[Guano Era|Peru]] to José María Alzamoro and Josefa Mayo. He studied at the [[College of Our Lady of Guadalupe]] and graduated in 1866. Later he entered the [[University of San Marcos]] and obtained a doctorate in jurisprudence.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://blog.pucp.edu.pe/blog/fernandotuesta/wp-content/uploads/sites/945/2016/05/1879-1881-Diputados.pdf |title=List of departments |publisher=blog.pucp.edu.pe |date= |access-date=2020-05-23}}</ref>

}}'''Edward Kessler''' [[Member of the Order of the British Empire|MBE]] (born 3 May 1963) is the Founder President of The Woolf Institute, a leading thinker in [[Interfaith dialogue|interfaith]] relations, primarily Jewish-Christian-Muslim Relations, a Fellow of [[St Edmund's College, Cambridge]] as well as Chair of both The Movement for Progressive Judaism and the Commission on Interfaith Relations.

title: Sino-Vietnamese vocabulary
diff_added:
'''Sino-Vietnamese vocabulary''' ({{langx|vi|từ Hán Việt}} ({{IPA|vi|tɯ̀ hǎn viə̂ˀt|ipa}}), [[Chữ Hán]]: 詞漢越, 'Chinese-Vietnamese words') is a layer of about 3,000 monosyllabic [[morpheme]]s of the [[Vietnamese language]] borrowed from [[Literary Chinese]] with consistent pronunciations based on [[Middle Chinese]]. Compounds using these morphemes are used extensively in cultural and technical vocabulary. Together with [[Sino-Korean vocabulary|Sino-Korean]] and [[Sino-Japanese vocabulary|Sino-Japanese]] vocabularies, Sino-Vietnamese has been used in the reconstruction of the sound categories of Middle Chinese. [[Samuel Martin (linguist)|Samuel Martin]] grouped the three together as "[[Sino-Xenic]]". There is also an '''Old Sino-Vietnamese''' layer consisting of a few hundred words borrowed individually from Chinese in earlier periods, which are treated by speakers as native words. More recent loans from southern [[Sinitic languages|Chinese languages]], usually names of foodstuffs such as {{lang|vi|lạp xưởng}} '[[Chinese sausage]]' (from [[Cantonese]] {{Lang-zh|s=腊肠|t=臘腸|cy=laahpchéung|labels=no|first=t}}), are not treated as Sino-Vietnamese but more direct borrowings.{{sfnp|Nguyễn|1997|p=79}}
The Old Sino-Vietnamese layer was introduced after the Chinese conquest of the kingdom of [[Nanyue]], including the northern part of Vietnam, in 111 BC. The influence of the Chinese language was particularly felt during the [[Eastern Han]] period (25–190 AD), due to increased Chinese immigration and official efforts to sinicize the territory.{{sfnp|Alves|2009b|pp=624–625}} This layer consists of roughly 400 words, which have been fully assimilated and are treated by Vietnamese speakers as native words.{{sfnp|Alves|2009b|pp=624, 628}} It has also been theorised that some Old-Sino-Vietnamese words came from a language shift from a population of Annamese [[Middle Chinese]] speakers that lived in the [[Red River Delta]], in northern Vietnam, to proto-Viet-Muong.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web |last=Phan |first=John |date=January 2013 |title=Lacquered Words: the Evolution of Vietnamese under Sinitic Influences from the 1st Century BCE to the 17th Century CE |url=https://ecommons.cornell.edu/bitstream/1813/33867/1/jdp49.pdf |website=Cornell |pages=298–301}}</ref>
|學士/学士 hohkish
|博士 bokish
Some Western names and words, approximated to Chinese languages often through Mandarin or in some cases approximated in Japanese and then borrowed into Chinese languages, were further approximated in Vietnamese. For example, Portugal is transliterated as {{lang|zh-Hant|葡萄牙}} ({{Lang-zh|p=Pútáoyá|cy=Pòuhtòuhngàh}}) and becomes ''Bồ Đào Nha'' in Vietnamese. England ({{Lang-zh|p=Yīnggélán|cy=Yīnggaaklàahn|t=英格蘭|labels=no}}) became ''Anh Cát Lợi'' ({{vi-nom|英吉利}}), shortened to ''Anh'' ({{vi-nom|英}}), while United States became ''Mỹ Lợi Gia'' ({{vi-nom|美利加}}), shortened to ''Mỹ'' ({{vi-nom|美}}). The formal name for the United States in Vietnamese is ''Hoa Kỳ'' ({{vi-nom|花旗}}); this is a for

[[Samuel Martin (linguist)|Samuel Martin]] grouped the three together as "[[Sino-Xenic]]". There is also an '''Old Sino-Vietnamese''' layer consisting of a few hundred words borrowed individually from Chinese in earlier periods, which are treated by speakers as native words. More recent loans from southern [[Sinitic languages|Chinese languages]], usually names of foodstuffs such as {{lang|vi|lạp xưởng}} '[[Chinese sausage]]' (from [[Cantonese]] {{Zh|s=腊肠|t=臘腸|cy=laahpchéung|labels=no|first=t}}), are not treated as Sino-Vietnamese but more direct borrowings.{{sfnp|Nguyễn|1997|p=79}}
The Old Sino-Vietnamese layer was introduced after the Chinese conquest of the kingdom of [[Nanyue]], including the northern part of Vietnam, in 111 BC. The influence of the Chinese language was particularly felt during the [[Eastern Han]] period (25–190 AD), due to increased Chinese immigration and official efforts to sinicize the territory.{{sfnp|Alves|2009b|pp=624–625}} This layer consists of roughly 400 words, which have been fully assimilated and are treated by Vietnamese speakers as native words.{{sfnp|Alves|2009b|pp=624, 628}} It has also been theorised that some Old-Sino-Vietnamese words came from a language shift from a population of Annamese [[Middle Chinese]] speakers that lived in the [[Red River Delta]], in northern Vietnam, to proto-Viet-Muong.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web |last=Phan |first=John |date=January 2013 |title=Lacquered Words: the Evolution of Vietnamese under Sinitic Influences from the 1st Century BCE to the 17th Century CE |url=https://ecommons.cornell.edu/bitstream/1813/33867/1/jdp49.pdf |website=Cornell |pages=298–301}}</ref>
|學士/学士 hohksih
|博士 boksih
Some Western names and words, approximated to Chinese languages often through Mandarin or in some cases approximated in Japanese and then borrowed into Chinese languages, were further approximated in Vietnamese. For example, Portugal is transliterated as {{lang|zh-Hant|葡萄牙}} ({{Zh|p=Pútáoyá|cy=Pòuhtòuhngàh}}) and becomes ''Bồ Đào Nha'' in Vietnamese. England ({{Zh|p=Yīnggélán|cy=Yīnggaaklàahn|t=英格蘭|labels=no}}) became ''Anh Cát Lợi'' ({{vi-nom|英吉利}}), shortened to ''Anh'' ({{vi-nom|英}}), while United States became ''Mỹ Lợi Gia'' ({{vi-nom|美利加}}), shortened to ''Mỹ'' ({{vi-nom|美}}). The formal name for the United States in Vietnamese is ''Hoa Kỳ'' ({{vi-nom|花旗}}); this is a former Sinitic nam

It will be challenging to form a government though.}}</ref> With GS and SDS winning 29 and 28 seats respectively, the left-leaning bloc led by Golob decreased to 40 [[Member of parliament|MPs]] and the right-leaning bloc led by Janša increased to 43 MPs.<ref name="Reuters1">{{Cite news |title=Liberal, populist parties tied in Slovenia's parliamentary vote |url=https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/slovenians-vote-tight-race-between-liberals-populist-right-2026-03-22/ |date=21 March 2026 |access-date=22 March 2026 |work=Reuters |first1=Fatos |last1=Bytyci |first2=Daria |last2=Sito-sucic |quote=Based on 99.45% of ballots counted, GS won 29 seats, trailed tightly by SDS with 28 seats. In third place was the right-wing coalition of [[New Slovenia]], [[Slovenian People's Party]] and [[Focus of Marko Lotrič|Focus]] taking 9.29% of the vote and winning a total of nine seats.
